_comment: MCP server config template for areas in AI-Skills-German-Law. Copy into <area>/.mcp.json and customize per area. MCP servers extend a skill with live tools: legal databases (Beck-Online, juris, openjur), court calendars, BGBl. lookup, etc. This file ships as documentation; per-area .mcp.json files are not currently shipped because no public MCP servers exist yet for Beck-Online or juris.

_example_beck_online._status: placeholder - no public Beck-Online MCP server exists yet

_example_juris._status: placeholder - no public juris MCP server exists yet

_example_openjur._status: openjur has a public web search; a local MCP wrapper can scrape responsibly

_example_gesetze_im_internet._status: public source, no auth needed; could be a thin wrapper around HTTP

_usage_in_skill: Reference an MCP server from inside a SKILL.md by saying e.g. 'Use the gesetze_im_internet MCP server to fetch the current text of § X' or 'If a beck_online MCP server is available, verify the citation via tool_call.'. The skill remains usable without MCP (model falls back to verification markers).
_note_on_203: An MCP server that ships Mandatsdaten to a remote service is itself a Mandatsgeheimnis-Empfänger and needs the same § 203/§ 43e BRAO contracts as any LLM provider. Self-hosted MCP servers are preferable for client work.
